etimesGot ‘LPG charges’ on your restaurant bill? Here’s what you can do right nowRestaurants can no longer add 'LPG charges' or similar fuel surcharges to your bill by default. The Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) has advised that these costs should be included in menu prices. Customers cannot be forced to pay extra unless it's voluntary. You can dispute such charges and report violations to consumer helplines.

etimes‘Better than English airports’: British traveller praises Lucknow Airport in viral videoA British traveler's video praising Lucknow Airport's modern facilities and efficient security has gone viral. Dave Flew highlighted the smooth experience, even comparing it favorably to some English airports. His positive review, particularly his tip for domestic electrical item checks, resonated with many, sparking conversations about India's improving airport standards and traveler satisfaction.

etimesChild calls ras malai ‘doodh wale kabab’, internet can’t get enoughA viral video features a child humorously renaming the popular Indian dessert, ras malai, as 'doodh wale kabab.' This unexpected description has resonated with millions online, sparking a wave of amusement and agreement. Many viewers are embracing the new moniker, with some sharing their own childhood names for the sweet, highlighting the dessert's universal appeal and the child's imaginative take.
